summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the abstraction in a Bridge. It owns an implementor through a pointer and forwards operations to it, so the abstraction and implementation hierarchies vary independently. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Bridge because {{className}} owns an implementor member via a smart pointer and forwards calls to it (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) — the abstraction-over-implementor seam.

bridge_implementor_member.note: The implementor reference. Holding it by pointer (not inheriting) is what lets the two hierarchies grow separately.

bridge_forwarded_call.note: A forwarded operation. The abstraction delegates real work to the implementor here.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.PIMPL: PIMPL hides one class's own implementation behind a private inner type for compilation firewalling. Bridge points at a separate, public implementor hierarchy meant to vary — same handle shape, different intent.
runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Adapter: Adapter converts an existing interface to a needed one. Bridge is designed up front to separate two dimensions that both evolve; it is not a retrofit.
